feature_name: Hub beta store sync and support scale refresh
scope_summary: Refreshes the website-attached hub beta build with the current Divergify app code, updated support scale messaging, corrected brand assets, current privacy/support pages, and Play Store-ready screenshots and graphics.

legal_and_regulatory.rationale: Public privacy and contact surfaces remain present and the website-attached app build keeps non-medical productivity framing.
evidence: privacy.html and contact.html remain part of the website update.
The attached hub beta build still exposes /legal/privacy and /legal/terms routes.

privacy_local_first_promise.rationale: The synced app build remains local-first by default and preserves Tinfoil Hat for local-only sessions.
evidence: Hub beta bundle includes current Tinfoil Hat copy and local-first privacy language.
Android release flow uses the hosted HTTPS backend only for optional assist calls, not silent background sync.

medical_scope_and_claims.rationale: The release stays inside productivity support language and does not introduce diagnosis or treatment claims.
evidence: Current in-app and website legal copy keeps the productivity-support disclaimer.
Support scale language describes pacing, task caps, nudges, and calmer presentation rather than medical effects.

behavioral_science_alignment.rationale: The refreshed support scale reduces ambiguity and makes the adaptive behavior more legible instead of more coercive.
evidence: Support now presents explicit baseline, medium, gentle, and high ranges.
Check-in UI explains the concrete behavior changes: sprint length, daily cap, nudge pace, and calmer defaults.

neurodivergence_research_alignment.rationale: The update continues to adapt workload to self-reported capacity and avoids rigid all-or-nothing pacing.
evidence: Support profile thresholds remain tied to user-reported stimulation value.
Higher-support states shorten focus windows and lower daily cap rather than forcing the same task load.

user_benefit_and_harm_check.rationale: Users get a clearer explanation of what support does, and the website-attached build now reflects the real current app instead of stale or incorrect assets.
evidence: Play screenshots were regenerated from the updated app state and current brand assets.
Website hub beta assets were rebuilt from the current app source before sync.

brand_voice_and_claims.rationale: The synced build uses the approved constellation branding and direct, human support language without inflated claims.
evidence: Current hub beta build includes the approved constellation mark and light wordmark assets.
Support copy now explains actual app behavior instead of abstract or AI-sounding language.

accessibility_and_inclusion.rationale: The support scale is easier to read and the attached build preserves reduced-motion and calmer-presentation controls.
evidence: Settings page now lists every support tier with concrete differences.
High support still auto-enables calmer presentation, while manual Shades and reduced-motion controls remain available.

security_and_reliability.rationale: The website-attached bundle and current Android release were rebuilt from source and verified locally before this ship gate.
evidence: npm test passed for the current app state.
npm run build passed for the current app state.
The Android release pipeline completed successfully after the support update.
scripts/build_app_into_site.sh rebuilt and copied the current dist into hub/beta.

monetization_and_dark_pattern_check.rationale: This ship updates support behavior, store assets, and website-attached app content only; it does not add any monetization surface or manipulative conversion flow.
evidence: No payment or subscription UI was added in the website sync.
No new paywall, upsell, or forced conversion prompts were introduced.

ship_decision.conditions: Ready to commit and push for website sync, with final manual device checks still recommended before Play submission.
